column3: The creation event for a document. Records the author, the timespan of creation, and the initial text content. May be influenced by existing documents.
Document Note: Document Creation Model Overview

column1: Document Note: Document Creation Model Overview
column3: This model captures the provenance of document creation. The E65 creation event records the author and timespan, and creates both the document entity and its initial text content. An optional influence link allows source or reference documents to be recorded. The model is intentionally source-agnostic; wiki page creation, CMS authoring, and manual attribution all map to the same structure.
The document that was created. Links back to the simple_document model.
An optional existing document that influenced the creation of this document.
The person or organisation who authored this document.
E41: Unique System Label or ID

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID
column3: Required within some documentation or database systems, such as ResearchSpace required label.
The scale of this author's involvement. Selected from a controlled list: initial_contribution, major_contribution, minor_contribution, micro_contribution, final_contribution.
The timespan of this author's activity on the document, from the earliest to latest recorded edit or contribution.

The initial text content of the document at the point of creation. Preserving this separately ensures the original content remains recoverable even after subsequent updates via the document_update model.
